kalarep: CRITICAL SHAPE NOTE: kohlrabi grows its edible bulb ABOVE the soil surface, on a short thick stem - NOT buried, NOT an onion, NOT a cabbage head. Smooth round-to-oval swollen bulb (pale green or purple), with thin leaf stalks radiating outward from the bulb's surface in a starburst pattern.
koperek: CRITICAL SHAPE NOTE: dill has extremely fine, thread-like, hair-thin feathery fronds - wispy, blue-green, on tall thin hollow stems. NOT broad or fan-shaped like parsley or cilantro.
koper ogrodowy: CRITICAL SHAPE NOTE: dill has extremely fine, thread-like, hair-thin feathery fronds - wispy, blue-green, on tall thin hollow stems. NOT broad or fan-shaped like parsley or cilantro.
pietruszk: CRITICAL SHAPE NOTE: parsley leaves are flat or tightly curled leaflets, broader and more solid than dill - NOT thread-like. Deep green wedge-shaped leaflets resembling small celery leaves. Root parsley: pale creamy-white tapered root like a parsnip, NOT orange like a carrot.
seler: CRITICAL SHAPE NOTE: celeriac has a large, knobby, rough-textured round root covered in small rootlets - NOT smooth like kohlrabi or turnip. Sits mostly above soil with celery-like leaf stalks on top.
brukiew: CRITICAL SHAPE NOTE: rutabaga is a round root, two-toned - purple top half above soil transitioning to pale yellow-cream bottom half below ground, smooth waxy skin, larger than a turnip.
por: CRITICAL SHAPE NOTE: leek is a long white-to-pale-green cylindrical stem, NOT a round bulb like onion or garlic, transitioning into flat, broad, dark green strap-like leaves fanning outward.
rabarbar: CRITICAL SHAPE NOTE: rhubarb has large, broad, heart-shaped dark green leaves on thick, celery-like reddish-pink stalks, growing in a dense clump - distinctly larger and coarser than chard or beet greens.
agrest: CRITICAL SHAPE NOTE: gooseberries are round-to-oval, often translucent and finely veined berries, growing individually or in small loose clusters along thorny branches.
porzeczk: CRITICAL SHAPE NOTE: currants are small round berries growing in tight, dangling clusters along smooth thornless stems, glossy opaque skin - NOT translucent like gooseberries.
